_readme: REFERENCE ONLY — these hooks are NOT active. They were active in ContentForge v3.0–v3.8 and removed in v3.9.0 because Claude Code plugin hooks fire globally on every session/operation regardless of working directory. The work each hook did is already covered elsewhere: hallucination checks live in agents/07-reviewer.md, brand-voice rules live inside each agent's instructions, and completion verification lives in the Quality Gate at the end of each phase. If you want to re-enable a hook for your own usage, copy the relevant entry into hooks.json — but understand it will fire across all your Claude Code sessions, not just ContentForge work.

SessionStart._note: Fires on every Claude Code launch in any project. Banner is noisy outside ContentForge work.

hooks.command: echo '✓ ContentForge v3.9 loaded — try /contentforge:brand-setup or /contentforge:create-content'

PreToolUse._note: Fires on EVERY Write|Edit in EVERY project. Even with the SKIP-if-not-ContentForge guard, the model is invoked on every file edit. Phase 7 reviewer (agents/07-reviewer.md) already runs this exact hallucination check at the right point in the pipeline.

hooks.prompt: Check the file path being written/edited. If the file is NOT content deliverable (article, blog, whitepaper, FAQ, research paper) and is NOT inside the ContentForge plugin directory, respond 'SKIP' immediately. Only proceed if this IS content being generated by ContentForge. If it is content: (1) Scan for hallucination indicators: statistics/percentages without source attribution, URLs with placeholder patterns (example.com, your-site.com, placeholder URLs), superlative claims ('best', '#1', 'leading') without substantiation, specific citations to studies or named sources that could be fabricated. (2) Flag any found with severity CRITICAL (for fabricated stats/URLs in headlines/CTAs) or WARNING (for unattributed claims in body text). (3) Include the specific text and a fix suggestion (add citation, remove claim, or use 'according to [verifiable source]'). Keep the check concise — flag only real hallucination risks. $ARGUMENTS

SubagentStart._note: Fires on EVERY subagent invocation in any project. Brand-voice rules already live inside each ContentForge agent's instructions (agents/*.md frontmatter and body). Re-injecting via global hook is redundant.

hooks.prompt: If this subagent is working on ContentForge content production, inject these rules: (1) All factual claims must cite a verified source — never fabricate statistics, study names, or URLs. (2) Follow the active brand voice and terminology from the brand profile. (3) Never use AI telltale phrases: 'delve', 'leverage', 'it's important to note', 'in today's rapidly evolving landscape'. (4) Maintain the confirmed title as the content anchor. (5) AI-generated images must be shown to user for approval before embedding. Otherwise respond 'SKIP'. $ARGUMENTS

Stop._note: Fires on EVERY Stop event in every project. Phase 7 reviewer + Quality Gate at end of each phase already perform this verification at the correct point.

hooks.prompt: If this task involved ContentForge content production, verify before completing: (1) All factual claims have source citations — flag any unsourced statistics. (2) No broken URLs or placeholder links (example.com, your-site.com). (3) Word count is within ±10% of target. (4) Brand voice compliance — no banned terms, correct terminology. (5) Quality score ≥7.0 if Phase 7 scoring was completed. (6) AI-generated images (if any) were approved by user. If any check fails, do NOT mark complete — list the issues and ask the user how to proceed. Otherwise respond 'PASS'. $ARGUMENTS
